01f86.content: Adopted an opt-in autoFields design for PHiveType. Inference is limited to constructor-backed fields, explicit PHiveField indexes win, inferred fields fill unused indexes deterministically in constructor order, and accessor annotations can override provisional inferred mappings without changing field order.

0dc2f.summary: Expanded example to cover hook and relation demos
0dc2f.content:
The example app now intentionally demonstrates two distinct PHive capabilities in one flow:
- Hooked cache behavior for encrypted and TTL-backed fields.
- Router relation behavior for parent-child storage, container reads, child-only deletion, and cascade deletion.

This keeps the example aligned with PHiveDynamicRouter's actual value instead of presenting it as plain typed CRUD over Hive boxes.

1a2f3.summary: PHiveStaticRouter uses BoxCollection, not Box<dynamic> with prefixed keys
1a2f3.content: PHiveStaticRouter was corrected to use Hive CE's BoxCollection / CollectionBox API rather than a single Box<dynamic> with namespaced key prefixes. Each registered type and ref store gets its own named CollectionBox within one BoxCollection, matching the naming conventions of PHiveDynamicRouter (type box: T.toString().toLowerCase(), ref box: __ref_ParentType_ChildType). All CRUD operations use async CollectionBox.get / put / delete. This gives web targets one IndexedDB database with multiple object stores instead of many independent databases.

2b3e4.summary: PHiveStaticRouter schema is initialization-locked, not compile-time
2b3e4.content: The language describing PHiveStaticRouter was corrected from 'compile-time' to 'initialization-locked'. The distinction matters: types and refs are registered at runtime by calling register<T> and createRef<T,P> on the router instance before ensureOpen(). Once ensureOpen() is called, BoxCollection.open() is invoked with the complete set of box names and no further registration is possible. This is a runtime initialization constraint, not a static or build-time constraint. Generator support (planned) would allow annotations to drive those registration calls automatically, but the locking mechanism itself is always runtime.

717cf.content:
The example now uses two router implementations intentionally:
- PHiveDynamicRouter handles the login/cache demo so generated adapters and field hooks continue to run through regular typed Hive boxes.
- PHiveStaticRouter handles the lesson/card relations demo so BoxCollection-backed ref behavior is demonstrated explicitly without mixing it into the hook path.
